note: Domain lets one Drupal install answer to many hostnames; each hostname is a `domain` config entity (entity type id `domain`, config prefix `domain.record.*`) and the DomainNegotiator resolves the active domain per request by matching the HTTP host. Easy cases are response-graded (no site changes). Medium cases stage KNOWN eval domain records on the live site (distinct `eval*` ids) and ask the agent to introspect them via drush; cleanup deletes them. Hard cases (persona drupal-expert) have the agent CREATE a real `domain` record; a verify script checks the live config entity (exit 0 = pass). All setup/reset/cleanup only ever touch eval-prefixed domain ids, never real domains. NB: the FIRST domain saved on a site is auto-marked default, so the create-default hard case seeds a placeholder default first to force an explicit promotion.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Domain (Domain Access) module installed, how is a single site domain stored? Give the entity type id, whether it is a config or content entity, and the config object prefix used for each record.
evals.expected_output: Each domain is a `domain` config entity (a ConfigEntityType, not content). Records are stored as config objects prefixed `domain.record.<id>` (config_prefix `record`).

evals.prompt: Using the Domain module's Drush commands, what single command creates a new domain record for the hostname example.com with the label "Example"? Give the command and the order of its two arguments.
evals.expected_output: drush domain:add example.com "Example" — the first argument is the hostname, the second is the human name; optional --scheme, --weight, --path-prefix options.

evals.prompt: In the Domain module, which service determines the 'active' domain for the current request, and what method returns the active domain object? Also name the match-type constant for an exact hostname match.
evals.expected_output: The `domain.negotiator` service (DomainNegotiatorInterface); getActiveDomain() returns the active DomainInterface. An exact host match is DomainNegotiatorInterface::DOMAIN_MATCHED_EXACT (=1).

evals.prompt: This Drupal 11 site already has some domain records configured. Using the Domain module, list the domain records currently configured on this site and give each one's hostname.
evals.expected_output: Two domain records are configured: eval_alpha (hostname eval-alpha.example.com, 'Eval Alpha') and eval_beta (hostname eval-beta.example.com, 'Eval Beta').

evals.prompt: On this live Drupal 11 site, several domain records exist. Which domain is the site's default (canonical) domain? Give its machine id and hostname.
evals.expected_output: The default domain is eval_two (hostname eval-two.example.com); eval_one exists but is not the default.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal 11 site with the Domain module installed, create a new domain record for the hostname news.eval.example.com with the label "Eval News". The end state I need: a `domain` config entity exists whose hostname is news.eval.example.com.
evals.expected_output: A `domain` config entity exists with hostname news.eval.example.com (label 'Eval News'), e.g. created via `drush domain:add news.eval.example.com "Eval News"` or the domain storage.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal 11 site (which already has a default domain), add a new domain record for hostname shop.eval.example.com labelled "Eval Shop" and make it the site's default (canonical) domain. The end state I need: a `domain` record for shop.eval.example.com exists and is marked as the default domain.
evals.expected_output: A `domain` config entity for shop.eval.example.com exists and is_default = TRUE (e.g. `drush domain:add shop.eval.example.com "Eval Shop"` then `drush domain:default <id>`, or ->saveDefault()).
